rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 26, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Hennessy Industries, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, motion for summary adjudication is continued to October 3, 2013 per defendant¿s representation that the parties have agreed to a continuance. While the parties mentioned October 2, 2013, the court is going to continue all motions set for October 2, 2013 to October 3, 2013. As the new hearing date will be within thirty days of the trial date, a stipulation and proposed order approving this continuance to October 3, 2013 must be presented to the court on September 26, 2013 at 9:45a.m. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: GEORGE MULVANNY VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 26, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Plaintiff¿s motion for leave to file second amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JAY WANLASS VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 26, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 7, Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Assuming without deciding that there is evidence creating a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing his asbestos-related illness. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: BENJAMIN J MOREHEAD et al VS. OWENS-ILLINOIS, INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, SEPTEMBER 26, 2013 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 9, Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.¿s motion to dismiss the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that Virginia is a suitable alternative forum because it did not show that all defendants are subject to jurisdiction there. (See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744; American Cemwood Corp. v. American Home Assurance Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 431.) Defendant¿s alternate motion to stay the action is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that California is a seriously inconvenient forum as the balance of private and public interest factors makes it just that the litigation remain in California. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
